data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Feb 19, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5 pm on Feb 19, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Bursa Opens Flat As Regional Markets Stabilise
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened cautiously, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) inching up 0.40 points, or 0.02%, to 1,752.51 at 9.02 am. Market sentiment was largely neutral, reflecting mixed cues from regional peers.

The broader market saw modest movements, with the FBM70 index slipping 18.38 points, or 0.10%, to 17,813.42, while the FBMEMAS and FBMSHA indices rose slightly to 12,862.14 and 12,405.68 respectively. Trading activity was concentrated in select counters, with NEXG leading volumes at 55.45 million shares, followed by Astro and MAG.

Top gainers in early trade included Panamy up 1.45% to RM7.71 and MPI adding 0.32% to RM30.88. On the flip side, UTD Plantations led losses, down 0.40% to RM29.84, alongside KGB, MCEMENT, and Vitrox.

The cautious start in Bursa mirrored a stabilising trend across Asia, following modest gains on Wall Street and a steady opening in Singapore’s Straits Times Index, which added 5.6 points to 5,007.15 amid moderate trading volumes.

Investors appeared to weigh corporate earnings, regional tech stocks, and lingering geopolitical tensions while keeping a close watch on domestic blue chips and high-volume counters.

Early trading suggests investors are taking a measured approach ahead of potential catalysts from both global markets and domestic economic data, with select sectors showing relative resilience while broader indices remain subdued.

data.title: SGX Opens Slightly Higher As Straits Times Index Gains 5.6 Points
data.description:
Singapore’s stock market opened modestly higher on Thursday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) rising 5.59 points, or 0.11%, to 5,007.15 as of 9.04am. Trading activity saw 72.94 million shares changing hands, valued at S$126.68 million, with 83 stocks advancing and 88 declining.

Among blue chips, Singtel led the gains, up 1.61% to S$5.06. Other key counters included DBS at S$57.95, OCBC Bank at S$21.62, and UOB at S$38.66. Singapore Airlines and ST Engineering traded at S$6.88 and S$10.19 respectively, while Yongnam Holdings / YZJ Shipbuilding stood at S$3.62.

Other indices showed mixed movements, with the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index at 1,510.73, the iEdge S-REIT Index at 1,105.93, and the iEdge APAC Financials Dividend Plus Index at 3,976.55.

Technology and regional trackers such as the iEdge Southeast Asia+ TECH Index and iEdge Emerging Asia Select 50 Index were at 4,867.79 and 1,508.77 respectively.

Derivatives activity included 9,650 contracts traded, with key futures levels showing FTSE China A50 at 14,865, Nikkei 225 at 56,890, and MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 465.55. Currency futures reflected a USD/SGD rate of 1.2659, while USD/CNH and KRW/USD futures were at 6.8899 and 0.6903 respectively.

Market participants remained cautious as early gains were modest, with attention on both domestic blue chips and regional index movements.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Dip Amid Tech Weakness and Local Concerns
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks closed lower on Feb 20, with the Hang Seng Index slipping 292.59 points, or 1.10%, to 26,413.35, as investors grew cautious amid weakness in technology and mainland Chinese shares. 

Selling pressure was concentrated in heavyweight tech counters and China-linked stocks, contributing to the market’s pullback.

The index traded in a range of 26,356.96 to 26,694.34, opening at 26,657.84 and closing near the day’s low, reflecting persistent investor caution. 

Market sentiment remained tentative, with participants trimming positions in growth-sensitive sectors amid ongoing global uncertainty and concerns over valuations following recent gains.

Analysts said the pullback underscores lingering caution in Hong Kong equities, with investors awaiting clearer signals on China’s economic recovery, tech regulations, and fresh catalysts to drive the next phase of market direction.

data.title: Stock Today: Nestlé Malaysia Edges Higher Amid Ice Cream Divestment Plans
data.description:
Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd shares edged up 0.46% to RM110.00 as of 12.02 pm, gaining RM0.50 from the previous close, on a traded volume of 19,800 shares.

The stock opened at RM110.00, hit an intraday high of RM110.10 and a low of RM109.30, with the last average closing price at RM109.50.

The modest uptick comes amid announcements that Nestlé Malaysia is exploring a potential sale of its local ice cream business to Froneri, the global joint venture with PAI Partners.

Analysts at MBSB Investment Bank Bhd have maintained a SELL rating on the stock, with an unchanged target price of RM94.50, noting that the divestment aligns with the parent company’s strategy under CEO Philipp Navratil to focus on four high-growth pillars: Coffee, Petcare, Nutrition and Food & Snacking.

The Malaysian operations include brands such as La Cremeria, Musang King sticks and Mat Kool, which were previously outside the scope of Froneri’s joint venture. The Board emphasised that the process remains at an early stage, involving due diligence, stakeholder engagement and regulatory compliance.

MBSB Research highlighted that a successful divestment could allow Nestlé Malaysia to concentrate on higher-margin core categories, potentially improving operational focus and medium-term earnings quality.

While financial details have not been disclosed, the company benefits from strong domestic brand equity, entrenched distribution networks and steady demand.

data.title: Nestlé Leads Top Gainers As PPB Slips In Early Trades
data.description:
Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd topped the gainers’ board, rising 20 sen to RM109.70 on volume of 924,000 shares.

F&N Holdings Bhd followed with an 18 sen gain to RM33.14, while Chin Teck Plantations Bhd climbed 16 sen to RM10.72. Maybank advanced 16 sen to RM12.22 on robust volume of 169.4 million shares, and Keyfield International Bhd rose 13 sen to RM1.62, rounding off the five biggest risers.

On the other end, PPB Group Bhd led the decliners, falling 24 sen to RM10.86 with 4.745 million shares traded. MPI slipped 20 sen to RM30.58, while KSL Holdings Bhd lost 19 sen to RM3.31. HLBank retreated 18 sen to RM24.00 on 14.838 million shares, and Atlan Holdings Bhd shed 13 sen to RM2.62, completing the top five losers.

The session reflected mixed sentiment as investors favoured consumer staples and banking heavyweights amid selective profit-taking in industrials and holdings counters, with broader indices showing cautious trading.

data.title: HSIF Rebound Signals Strong Base, RHB Keeps Long Call
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) is maintaining its long position call on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) after the contract staged a firm rebound last Monday.

The HSIF climbed 270 points to close at 26,800 points after opening at 26,500 points. It touched an intraday low of 26,246 points before advancing steadily to a high of 26,810 points, eventually settling near the day’s peak and forming a long bullish candlestick.

RHB Research said the latest price action suggests that a strong support base has formed around the 26,400-point zone. As long as the index holds above this level, the bulls are expected to retain the technical advantage. A follow-through rebound could push the HSIF above its 20-day simple moving average, reinforcing the emerging bullish structure.

However, a break below 26,400 points would invalidate the positive setup and open the door to further downside movement.

For now, the research house is keeping a positive trading bias. Traders are advised to hold the long position initiated at 21,416 points on Apr 14, 2025, while managing risk with a revised stop-loss set at 26,400 points.

Immediate support is seen at 26,400, followed by 25,500. On the upside, resistance levels are pegged at 28,600 points and 29,100 points.

data.title: Singapore Exchange STI Rises 0.32% To 5,017.60 Amid Mixed Breadth
data.description:
Singapore stocks nudged higher on February 20 as the Straits Times Index (STI) gained modestly amid mixed regional performances, buoyed by shipping gains despite more decliners overall.

The STI rose 16.04 points, or 0.32%, to close at 5,017.60, reversing some prior volatility while staying near recent highs.

Market turnover totalled 1.41 billion shares valued at S$1.98 billion. Decliners slightly outnumbered advancers, with 304 stocks falling compared with 280 gainers, reflecting a mixed broader market performance despite the STI’s advance.

Across the broader market, the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index ended at 1,521.21, while the iEdge S-REIT Index stood at 1,103.22. Other indices, including the iEdge-OCBC Singapore Low Carbon Select 40 Capped Index and the iEdge APAC Financials Dividend Plus Index, also closed in positive territory.

Analysts said the modest gain suggests cautious optimism, with investors monitoring global interest rate expectations and regional economic developments. Market participants expect near-term consolidation as traders assess external risks and earnings momentum.

data.title: Malaysia Gold Shines Despite Stronger Dollar, Up 38% In Six Months
data.description:
Gold prices in Malaysia continued to edge higher, tracking firm global bullion prices despite a stronger US dollar.

According to data from GoldPrice.org, gold was priced at RM19,560.75 per ounce as of Feb 20, 2026 (02:54 NY time), up RM100.62 on the day. Gold per gram stood at RM628.89, gaining RM3.23, while gold per kilogram rose to RM628,892.25, up RM3,234.69.

Daily, gold prices in ringgit terms increased 0.49%. Over the past six months, prices have surged 37.98%, while the one-year gain stands at 49.95%. Year-to-date, gold is up 15.1% in US dollar terms.

However, global sentiment remains cautious. A Reuters article said gold was heading for a weekly decline of about 1% as the US dollar strengthened to a near one-month high, supported by stronger-than-expected economic data and a more hawkish Federal Reserve outlook. Spot gold was last seen around US$5,000 per ounce.

Markets are awaiting key US Personal Consumption Expenditure (PCE) inflation data for further clues on the Federal Reserve’s rate path. Non-yielding assets like gold typically perform better in a lower interest rate environment.

RHB Investment Bank Bhd noted that COMEX gold remains above its 20-day simple moving average, with technical indicators suggesting bullish momentum remains intact. The research house maintained a positive trading bias, with immediate resistance seen at US$5,500 and support at US$4,800.

Silver prices in Malaysia also advanced, rising 1.35% on the day, although the metal remains down 21.84% over the past 30 days.

Despite near-term volatility, gold’s strong medium- to long-term performance continues to underpin investor interest in the precious metal.

data.title: Late Bargain Hunting Lifts Bursa
data.description:
Late bargain hunting in selected heavyweights, mainly financial counters, has lifted Bursa Malaysia to end marginally higher on Friday.

At 5 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) inched up 0.72 of a point, or 0.04 per cent, to 1,752.83 from Thursday’s close of 1,752.11.

The benchmark had opened 0.68 of-a-point higher at 1,752.79, and moved between 1,746.08 and 1,754.20 throughout the trading session.

However, the broader market was negative with losers thumping gainers 702 to 380, while 519 counters were unchanged, 1,157 counters untraded and 24 suspended.

Turnover was slightly lower at 2.03 billion units worth RM2.20 billion compared to 2.04 billion units worth RM2.52 billion on Thursday.

IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the benchmark index’s early weakness was driven by profit-taking following yesterday’s 0.62 per cent gain and a softer risk tone amid heightened geopolitical uncertainty.

“On a sectoral basis, financial counters led the gainers, while the consumer products and services sector weighed on overall performance amid profit-taking and more defensive positioning.

“From a flow perspective, foreign investors have turned net buyers, signalling continued global interest in Malaysian equities despite subdued trading activity,” he told Bernama.

Among heavyweights, Maybank rose 16 sen to RM12.22, while Public Bank lost three sen to RM5.03. CIMB was two sen higher at RM8.49, while Tenaga Nasional dropped 10 sen to RM14.16 and IHH Healthcare fell seven sen to RM8.84.

On the most active list, Astro Malaysia added 2.5 sen to 10 sen. Zetrix AI lost two sen to 83.5 sen, while Tanco advanced two sen to RM1.46. Bina Puri and Velesto remained unchanged at 30 sen and 31.5 sen respectively.

Besides Maybank, other top gainers included Nestle, which rose by 20 sen to RM109.70, Fraser and Neave added 18 sen to RM33.14, Chin Teck Plantations bagged 16 sen to RM10.72, and Keyfield International advanced 13 sen to RM1.62.

Leading decliners were PPB, which lost 24 sen to RM10.86, while Malaysian Pacific Industries shed 20 sen to RM30.58, KSL Holdings narrowed 19 sen to RM3.31, Hong Leong Bank fell 18 sen to RM24 and Atlan Holdings tumbled 13 sen to RM2.62.

On the index board, the FBM Top 100 Index fell 10.58 points to 12,667.89, the FBM Emas Index dropped 13.69 points to 12,846.78, the FBM Mid 70 Index narrowed 86.10 points to 17,745.70, the FBM Emas Shariah Index lost 35.50 points to 12,368.29 and the FBM ACE Index eased 6.39 points to 4,822.12.

Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index edged up 50.57 points to 21,615.67, the Plantation Index edged up by 17.67 points to 8,419.98, the Energy Index inched up 1.77 points to 775.88, while the Industrial Products and Services Index was 0.44 of a point lower at 178.06.

 The Main Market volume inched down to 1.27 billion units worth RM2.04 billion from 1.35 billion units worth RM2.37 billion on Thursday.

Warrant turnover expanded to 514.93 million units worth RM62.36 million from 467.07 million units worth RM53.90 million previously.

The ACE Market volume perked up to 248.54 million units valued at RM95.89 million from 227.62 million units valued at RM97.82 million, yesterday.

Consumer products and services counters accounted for 174.75 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (176.47 million), construction (104.18 million), technology (182.29 million), financial services (82.22 million), property (138.80 million), plantation (16.84 million), real estate investment trusts (17.97 million), closed-end fund (23,100), energy (111.16 million), healthcare (56.81 million), telecommunications and media (152.90 million), transportation and logistics (22.68 million), utilities (30.12 million) and business trusts (14,000).

Bernama

data.title: Stock Picks: SunCon And Malakoff
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Sunway Construction Group Bhd (SunCon) and Malakoff Corp Bhd as potential technical trading opportunities, citing improving price momentum and breakout signals.

SunCon’s uptrend may be extended after breaching its RM6.40 resistance on heightened volume. RHB Research noted that the formation of a long bullish candlestick confirms strong buying interest and signals that bullish momentum is accelerating.

Following the breakout, the research house expects follow-through price action towards RM6.70, with the next upside target at the RM7 level. However, a decline below the RM6.10 support would invalidate the bullish setup and suggest a resumption of the prior correction phase.

Meanwhile, Malakoff is poised to extend its rebound after closing above its 21-day simple moving average on a bullish candlestick.

The stock has climbed past its short-term moving average and broken above the RM0.82 resistance, indicating a shift in the near-term trend to the upside. If momentum persists, RHB Research anticipates further gains towards RM0.88, followed by RM0.94.

That said, a pullback below the RM0.76 support level would negate the bullish structure and potentially trigger another round of correction.

data.title: Wall Street Closes Up As Tariff Relief Offsets Weak Q4 GDP
data.description:
US stocks advanced on Feb 20 after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs under the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act, easing trade uncertainty despite weak Q4 GDP data showing 1.4% growth.

Reuters reported that the S&P 500 rose 0.7% after a choppy session, recovering from early losses triggered by disappointing fourth-quarter growth figures.

Wall Street had opened lower but pushed into positive territory as investors digested the court’s 6-3 ruling that a 1977 emergency powers law does not authorise the president to impose tariffs.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average and Nasdaq Composite also ended higher, reflecting cautious optimism that the removal of tariffs could ease price pressures and strengthen expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts later this year.

Many analysts stated markets had largely anticipated the ruling by the US Supreme Court, resulting in a relatively muted reaction.

However, uncertainty remains elevated after the administration of Donald Trump signalled it could pursue alternative legal avenues to reinstate trade levies, keeping investors cautious about the longer-term policy outlook.

That caution was reinforced by fresh economic data showing the US economy expanded at an annualised rate of 1.4% in the October-to-December quarter, falling short of the 2.5% pace forecast by analysts.

Economists noted that a prolonged federal government shutdown during the period likely weighed on output, tempering concerns about underlying momentum but underscoring signs of cooling growth.

Overseas, markets painted a mixed picture. In Asia, Hong Kong stocks declined as trading resumed after a three-day Lunar New Year break, while Tokyo also closed lower. In contrast, South Korea’s Kospi surged 2.3% to a record high, buoyed by gains in defence contractors including Hanwha Aerospace.

European equities were firmer, supported by data showing eurozone business activity accelerated in February, signalling improving economic stability. London’s FTSE 100 and Paris’s CAC 40 both notched fresh record highs.

In commodities, oil prices moved sideways after rallying earlier in the week on US suggestions of potential military action against Iran. President Trump said he was considering options after warning Tehran of consequences if a nuclear deal was not reached within an extended deadline.

Against this backdrop of shifting trade policy, moderating growth and geopolitical tensions, market participants expect volatility to persist as investors reassess inflation trends and the prospects for monetary easing later this year.

data.title: Stock Today: MBM Resources Slips 1.13% In Active Trade
data.description:
Shares of MBM Resources Bhd declined in afternoon trade, falling 1.13% to RM5.26 as of 3.09 pm, based on prices delayed by 15 minutes.

The stock shed 6 sen from its previous close of RM5.32, with 67,700 shares changing hands. MBM opened at RM5.30 and touched an intraday high of RM5.30 before easing to a low of RM5.24.

At the time of writing, the counter saw buying interest at RM5.25 with 5,700 shares on the bid, while 4,300 shares were offered at RM5.26.

MBM Resources, listed on the Main Market under the Consumer Products & Services sector, has traded within a range of RM4.50 to RM5.75 over the past six months.

The latest movement reflects mild selling pressure during the session, with the stock hovering near its intraday low.

data.title: Bursa Extends Upward Momentum
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia continued its upward trend, closing higher on Thursday due to positive market sentiment driven by stronger-than-expected macroeconomic data.

The bourse also benefitted from gains on Wall Street overnight and firmer regional markets.

The market was closed on Feb 17 and Feb 18 for the Chinese New Year holidays.

At 5 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose by 10.85 points, or 0.62 per cent, to 1,752.11 compared with Monday’s close of 1,741.26.

The benchmark index had opened 6.82 points higher at 1,748.08, and moved between 1,755.08 and 1,743.63 throughout the trading session.

The broader market was also positive with gainers thumping losers 748 to 288, while 483 counters were unchanged, 1,218 counters untraded, and 11 suspended.

Turnover was higher at 2.04 billion units, worth RM2.52 billion, compared to 1.81 billion units, worth RM1.81 billion, on Monday.

IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said most regional equity markets closed higher, reinforcing the constructive risk tone that carried into the local benchmark index.

“Major indices finished in positive territory, reflecting broad-based buying interest. Among benchmark constituents, banking stocks led the gainers on renewed risk appetite, while utilities counters lagged amid sector rotation.

“On a sectoral basis, the energy index outperformed, advancing by more than two per cent, driven by a surge of over seven per cent in selected green energy stocks,” he told Bernama.

Mohd Sedek also said that the United States markets ended higher in the previous session as manufacturing resilience re-entered the narrative, with stronger-than-expected durable goods orders and industrial production.

“On the economic front, today’s release of Malaysia’s January consumer price index (CPI) data showed headline inflation steady at 1.6 per cent year-on-year, with core inflation unchanged at 2.3 per cent, reflecting a balance of opposing price pressures rather than an absence of movement.

“This upward pressure was offset by declining transport and energy prices, which moved into negative territory during the month, exerting a countervailing influence on the headline index,” he added.

Among the heavyweights, Maybank gained 12 sen to RM12.06, Public Bank rose 10 sen to RM5.06, CIMB and IHH Healthcare added nine sen each to RM8.47 and RM8.91, respectively, while Tenaga Nasional fell 16 sen to RM14.26.

On the most active list, Zetrix AI shed half a sen to 85.5 sen, Tanco and Capital A advanced one sen each to RM1.44 and 60.5 sen, respectively, Sime Darby climbed six sen to RM2.40, while 99 Speed Mart slipped 23 sen to RM3.50.

As for the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries climbed 80 sen to RM30.78, Allianz Malaysia grew 40 sen to RM22.66, Petronas Dagangan bagged 38 sen to RM21, Hong Leong Financial expanded 32 sen to RM22.32, and Kelington garnered 31 sen to RM5.11.

Besides 99 Speed Mart and Tenaga Nasional, other top decliners included Southern Acids, which slipped 17 sen to RM3.28, Dutch Lady Milk lost 14 sen to RM33.14, and Teo Guan Lee dipped seven sen to 83 sen.

On the index board, the FBM Top 100 Index expanded 99.25 points to 12,678.47, the FBM Emas Index notched up 103.92 points to 12,860.47, the FBM Mid 70 Index increased 233.21 points to 17,831.80, the FBM Emas Shariah Index improved 94.69 points to 12,403.79, and the FBM ACE Index added 55.72 points to 4,828.51.

Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index was up by 206.68 points to 21,565.10, the Plantation Index grew 41.96 points to 8,402.31, the Energy Index rose 19.29 points to 774.11, and the Industrial Products and Services Index inched up 1.24 points to 178.50.

 The Main Market volume climbed up to 1.35 billion units worth RM2.37 billion from 1.15 billion units worth RM1.68 billion on Monday.

Warrant turnover expanded to 467.07 million units worth RM53.90 million from 401.15 million units worth RM49.71 million previously.

The ACE Market volume advanced to 227.62 million units valued at RM97.82 million from 259.17 million units valued at RM80.65 million on Monday.

Consumer products and services counters accounted for 224.63 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (147.27 million), construction (117.67 million), technology (275.17 million), financial services (108.28 million), property (159.68 million), plantation (16.13 million), real estate investment trusts (26.36 million), closed-end fund (nil), energy (94.67 million), healthcare (56.44 million), telecommunications and media (47.57 million), transportation and logistics (43.29 million), utilities (27.71 million), and business trusts (735,400).

data.title: Stock Today: CIMB Inches Up Amid Indonesia Stake Talks
data.description:
Shares of CIMB Group Holdings Bhd were trading higher on Thursday afternoon, gaining 0.84% as investors considered the potential divestment of its Indonesian unit.

As of 3.59 pm, the counter was last done at RM8.45, up seven sen from the previous close of RM8.38. The stock had touched a session high of RM8.48 and a low of RM8.41, with total traded volume at 130.48 million shares (’00 basis).

The market’s focus remains on CIMB’s 92.4% holding in PT Bank CIMB Niaga TBK, as Indonesian regulators plan to raise the minimum free float requirement on the Indonesia Stock Exchange from 7.5% to 15%, ahead of an MSCI reassessment in May.

Analysts suggest CIMB may reduce its stake by at least 7.4%, potentially generating one-off proceeds of around RM810 million based on Niaga’s current market capitalisation of approximately RM10.9 billion.

Despite Niaga historically contributing about 25% to CIMB’s profit before tax, the impact of a potential stake reduction is expected to be minimal, with projected declines of 1.2% and 2.0% for FY26 and FY27 respectively. Analysts note that any divestment proceeds could be used to expand the loan book or enhance shareholder returns, complementing the bank’s RM2 billion capital return plan through 2027.

Currency risk remains a factor, with every 1% depreciation of the Indonesian rupiah against its year-to-date average estimated to reduce CIMB’s profit before tax by about 0.3%.

data.title: Wall Street Ends Lower Amid Private Equity Slump And Tech Weakness
data.description:
Wall Street closed lower on Thursday as losses in private equity firms and dips in major tech and retail stocks outweighed earnings-driven gains in industrials.

The S&P 500 fell 0.28% to 6,861.89 points, the Nasdaq declined 0.31% to 22,682.73, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 0.54% to 49,395.16.

Private equity stocks were hit after Blue Owl Capital announced it would sell US$1.4 billion in assets and freeze redemptions at one of its funds to manage debt and return capital.

Shares of Blue Owl tumbled 6%, while peers including Apollo Global Management, Ares, KKR & Co and Carlyle Group fell between 1.9% and 5.2%, reflecting concerns over credit quality and lenders’ exposure to software stocks.

Tech and retail stocks also weighed on sentiment. Apple dipped 1.4%, the largest drag on the S&P 500, while Walmart dropped 1.4% after new CEO John Furner unveiled a conservative fiscal 2027 forecast and a US$30 billion share buyback plan.

AI-related technology stocks have faced pressure amid doubts that heavy investments are translating into meaningful revenue or profit growth.

Industrial stocks were a bright spot. Deere & Co surged 11.6% after raising its annual profit forecast and beating first-quarter estimates.

Omnicom jumped 15% following stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter revenue, while Carvana fell almost 8% after missing profit expectations. Software provider EPAM Systems plunged 17% after issuing a cautious first-quarter outlook.

The S&P 500 energy index gained 0.6% on rising crude oil prices amid heightened US-Iran tensions, while the financial index slipped 0.9%.

Declining stocks outnumbered advancing ones on the S&P 500 by a 1.5-to-one ratio, with 27 new highs and six new lows. Nasdaq recorded 62 new highs and 146 new lows. Trading volume was lighter than usual, with 16.4 billion shares changing hands versus the 20-session average of 20.5 billion.

Investors also digested the Fed’s latest policy minutes, showing a split among policymakers on the future path of rates.

Attention now turns to Friday’s Personal Consumption Expenditures report for clues on inflation and the central bank’s next moves, with interest-rate markets currently pricing a 50% chance of a rate cut by June.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Rallies Midday As All Major Indices Ride Broad-Based Buying
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended gains at midday, with all major indices advancing on broad-based buying.

The FBMKLCI rose 10.85 points or 0.62% to 1,752.11, while the FBM70 gained 198.16 points (+1.13%) to 17,796.75. The FBM Emas index added 97.69 points (+0.77%) to 12,854.24, and the FBM Shariah index increased 103.87 points (+0.84%) to 12,412.97. The F4GBM also climbed 8.21 points (+0.80%) to 1,036.20.

Trading activity remained focused on mid-cap and small-cap stocks. ZETRIX [0138] led in volume with 105 million shares, closing at RM0.84, slightly down 0.01, while TANCO [2429] inched up 0.01 to RM1.44 on 27.8 million shares. BPURI [5932] slipped 0.005 to RM0.295, 99SMART [5326] fell 0.17 to RM3.56, and CAPITALA [5099] gained 0.015 to RM0.61.

Top gainers included MPI [3867] up 0.92 to RM30.90 and Nestlé [4707] rising 0.70 to RM110.00, while BIPORT [5032] lost 0.20 to RM5.30 and DLADY [3026] dipped 0.20 to RM33.08.

Market sentiment remained cautiously optimistic, led by consumer staples and industrial stocks, as investors positioned ahead of corporate earnings and global cues, keeping all major indices in positive territory by midday.

data.title: Stock Today: Malakoff Rises Over 3% As Plant Concessions Extended To 2029
data.description:
Malakoff Corp Bhd climbed in afternoon trade on Thursday, rising 2.5 sen or 3.11% to 83 sen as at 2.45 pm, amid investor reaction to the extension of key power plant concessions.

The utility counter opened at 80.5 sen and moved between 80.5 sen and an intraday high of 84.5 sen, with some 2.08 million shares changing hands.

The stock’s latest price stood above its last done price of 80.5 sen previously, reflecting renewed buying interest. The buy queue was at 83 sen against a sell price of 83.5 sen.

The gain follows news that Malakoff secured extensions for three power plants under its subsidiaries until Dec 31, 2029. The assets comprise Segari Energy Ventures Sdn Bhd in Perak with a capacity of 1,303 megawatts, GB3 Sdn Bhd in Perak with 429 megawatts, and Prai Power Sdn Bhd in Penang with 350 megawatts.

The extensions strengthen earnings visibility for the independent power producer by ensuring continued operations of the combined 2,082 megawatts capacity over the next several years. Investors appeared to welcome the improved medium-term revenue certainty, pushing the stock higher during the session.

Malakoff, listed on the Main Market under the utilities sector, has seen its share price trend within the 70 sen to RM1.20 range over the past year. Thursday’s advance places the counter closer to the upper end of its recent trading band as the market digests the concession developments.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Against US Dollar Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar on Friday as traders turned cautious amid escalating geopolitical tensions, an analyst said.

At 8 am, the local currency rose to 3.8965/9160 against the greenback from 3.9045/9125 at Thursday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd Chief Economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said risk-off sentiment had become evident in global financial markets as traders focused on developments surrounding the US-Iran nuclear deal.

“US President Donald Trump has set a 10-day deadline to conclude negotiations. At the same time, there are reports of US military forces building up in the Middle East,” he said to Bernama.

He added that the US dollar-ringgit pair was expected to trade between RM3.91 and RM3.93 as markets remained wary of further escalation.

Meanwhile, Mohd Afzanizam said recent US data showed imports had declined for five consecutive months, falling 2.6 per cent year-on-year in December 2025, while the trade deficit narrowed to US$70.3 billion from US$96.9 billion in December 2024.

He said the data suggested softer domestic demand had dampened import growth in the US economy.

“On the other hand, initial jobless claims fell slightly to 219,000 last week from 220,000 previously.

“However, claims have been trending higher since early January, indicating the labour market may be softening, with continuing claims remaining at an elevated level,” he said.

At the opening, the ringgit traded higher against a basket of major and ASEAN currencies.

It strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.5126/5250 from 2.5221/5275 at Thursday’s close, rose versus the euro to 4.5880/6103 from 4.6073/6168, and appreciated against the British pound to 5.2469/2725 from 5.2730/2838.

Against its ASEAN peers, the ringgit advanced versus the Singapore dollar to 3.0732/0887 from 3.0817/0882, gained against the Thai baht to 12.4944/5657 from 12.5196/5521, edged up against the Indonesian rupiah to 230.8/232.0 from 231.1/231.6, and rose versus the Philippine peso to 6.72/6.76 from 6.73/6.75 previously.

Bernama
